Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=draft}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神|12=draft}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=draft}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|12=draft}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|12=draft}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|12=draft}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}}

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:片山日子神社 up to rev 98523471
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=片山日子神社 |所在地=岡山県瀬戸内市長船町土師799 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|48.47|N|134|6|20.51|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=片山日子神 |創建=伝・[[天喜]]3年（[[1055年]]） |社格=式内社、郷社 |例祭=春季大祭：5月20日
秋季大祭：10月10日 |本殿=三間社入母屋造 |画像=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|片山日子神社]] }} '''片山日子神社'''（かたやまひこじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[長船町]]にある[[神社]]。祭神は片山日子神（片山日子命）。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]。[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 概要 == [[延喜式神名帳]]に従二位片山日子神社とあり、[[伊勢国]][[鈴鹿郡]][[片山神社 (亀山市)|片山神社]]と同神とされている。 祭神は片山日子神であるが、社伝によれば「片山日子神とは片山に坐す[[吉備津彦命|吉備津日子命]]の略称である」としている。また、『備前式社考』によれば従三位賀多山大明神・賀多山大神とあり、『神祇志料』によれば賀茂片山御子神、『備前国志』・『神社覈録』では[[大山咋神]]、『吉備温故録』では[[天日方奇日方命]]となっている。 創建は不明であるが、古来、神社の南にある神山（甲山）に鎮座していた。[[平安時代]]中期の[[天喜]]3年（[[1055年]]）に現在の地に遷座したと伝えられている『備陽國志』。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）郷社となる。明治40年（[[1907年]]）神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定される。 == 文化財 == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|算額]] ;瀬戸内市指定重要文化財 * 片山日子神社算額 : 明治6年（1873年）10月に[[邑久郡]][[笠加村]]箕輪（現・瀬戸内市[[邑久町]]箕輪）の入江信順が開いた算学塾「萬春亭」の門下生16名が各1題の幾何の問題を解いた[[算額]]である。額縁には[[龍]]の彫刻が施してあり、これは玉津村尻海（現・瀬戸内市邑久町尻海）の彫刻師である井上幽雪斎・鶴峰父子の手によるものである。縦180[[センチメートル|cm]]、横182cm、額縁材は[[ケヤキ]]、板材は[[スギ]]である。[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）3月31日旧長船町指定。 * 片山日子神社だんじり : 明治15年（[[1882年]]）5月15日に[[児島郡]]北浦村（現・[[岡山市]][[南区 (岡山市)|南区]]北浦）の大工・片山吉衛忠久が製作。同年5月19日の春季大祭で披露巡行された。翌年からは秋季大祭で巡行された。現在は秋季大祭の夜に境内で巡行されている。総ケヤキ造、総高3.4[[メートル|m]]、全長3.4m、全幅2m。平成14年（[[2002年]]）4月16日旧長船町指定。 == 参考文献 == * 邑久郡教育会/刊 『邑久郡誌 第二編』 小林久磨雄/編 1915年 * 現地説明板 == 注釈 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 片山日子神社]岡山県神社庁HP {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かたやまひこしんしや}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[片山日子神社]] to [[Katayama Hiko Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=片山日子神社 |所在地=岡山県瀬戸内市長船町土師799 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|41|48.47|N|134|6|20.51|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=片山日子神 |創建=伝・[[天喜]]3年（[[1055年]]） |社格=式内社、郷社 |例祭=春季大祭：5月20日
秋季大祭：10月10日 |本殿=三間社入母屋造 |画像=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|片山日子神社]] }} '''片山日子神社'''（かたやまひこじんじゃ）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]][[長船町]]にある[[神社]]。祭神は片山日子神（片山日子命）。[[延喜式神名帳|式内小社]]。[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 概要 == [[延喜式神名帳]]に従二位片山日子神社とあり、[[伊勢国]][[鈴鹿郡]][[片山神社 (亀山市)|片山神社]]と同神とされている。 祭神は片山日子神であるが、社伝によれば「片山日子神とは片山に坐す[[吉備津彦命|吉備津日子命]]の略称である」としている。また、『備前式社考』によれば従三位賀多山大明神・賀多山大神とあり、『神祇志料』によれば賀茂片山御子神、『備前国志』・『神社覈録』では[[大山咋神]]、『吉備温故録』では[[天日方奇日方命]]となっている。 創建は不明であるが、古来、神社の南にある神山（甲山）に鎮座していた。[[平安時代]]中期の[[天喜]]3年（[[1055年]]）に現在の地に遷座したと伝えられている『備陽國志』。 [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）郷社となる。明治40年（[[1907年]]）神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定される。 == 文化財 == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|算額]] ;瀬戸内市指定重要文化財 * 片山日子神社算額 : 明治6年（1873年）10月に[[邑久郡]][[笠加村]]箕輪（現・瀬戸内市[[邑久町]]箕輪）の入江信順が開いた算学塾「萬春亭」の門下生16名が各1題の幾何の問題を解いた[[算額]]である。額縁には[[龍]]の彫刻が施してあり、これは玉津村尻海（現・瀬戸内市邑久町尻海）の彫刻師である井上幽雪斎・鶴峰父子の手によるものである。縦180[[センチメートル|cm]]、横182cm、額縁材は[[ケヤキ]]、板材は[[スギ]]である。[[平成]]2年（[[1990年]]）3月31日旧長船町指定。 * 片山日子神社だんじり : 明治15年（[[1882年]]）5月15日に[[児島郡]]北浦村（現・[[岡山市]][[南区 (岡山市)|南区]]北浦）の大工・片山吉衛忠久が製作。同年5月19日の春季大祭で披露巡行された。翌年からは秋季大祭で巡行された。現在は秋季大祭の夜に境内で巡行されている。総ケヤキ造、総高3.4[[メートル|m]]、全長3.4m、全幅2m。平成14年（[[2002年]]）4月16日旧長船町指定。 == 参考文献 == * 邑久郡教育会/刊 『邑久郡誌 第二編』 小林久磨雄/編 1915年 * 現地説明板 == 注釈 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 片山日子神社]岡山県神社庁HP {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かたやまひこしんしや}} [[Category:岡山県の神社]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]] [[Category:備前国の式内社]] [[Category:岡山県の旧郷社]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Katayamahiko Shrine |location=Setouchi City, Nagafune-cho, Haji 799, Okayama Prefecture |Position={{coord|34|41|48.47|N|134|6|20.51|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=Katayamahiko-no-Kami |Foundation=Traditionally Tenki 3 (1055) |ShrineRank=Shikinaisha, Gōsha |AnnualFestival=Spring Grand Festival: May 20
Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Katayamahiko Shrine |location=Setouchi City, Nagafune-cho, Haji 799, Okayama Prefecture |Position={{coord|34|41|48.47|N|134|6|20.51|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=Katayamahiko-no-Kami |Foundation=Traditionally Tenki 3 (1055) |ShrineRank=Shikinaisha, Gōsha |AnnualFestival=Spring Grand Festival: May 20
Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}}

Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]]

Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setouchi, Okayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|qq=}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sangaku|qq=}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|qq=}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaura|qq=}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]]

It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Katayamahiko Shrine |location=Setouchi City, Nagafune-cho, Haji 799, Okayama Prefecture |Position={{coord|34|41|48.47|N|134|6|20.51|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=Katayamahiko-no-Kami |Foundation=Traditionally Tenki 3 (1055) |ShrineRank=Shikinaisha, Gōsha |AnnualFestival=Spring Grand Festival: May 20
Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setouchi, Okayama|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qq=}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|qq=}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sangaku|qq=}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|qq=}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaura|qq=}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]]

Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]], and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|qq=}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This [[:en:Sangaku|sangaku]], dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|qq=}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from [[:en:Kitaura|Kitaura]], Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]]

The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]], and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|qq=}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This [[:en:Sangaku|sangaku]], dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|qq=}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from [[:en:Kitaura|Kitaura]], Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]]

Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]], and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|qq=}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This [[:en:Sangaku|sangaku]], dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|qq=}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from [[:en:Kitaura|Kitaura]], Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Katayamahiko-jina}}

Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]], and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja||1873年|1=1873|2=ja|3=1873年|lt=Meiji 6|lt_ja=明治6年||qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 6|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1873年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This [[:en:Sangaku|sangaku]], dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|qq=}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from [[:en:Kitaura|Kitaura]], Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{Commons category|Katayamahiko-jina}}

The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]], and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This [[:en:Sangaku|sangaku]], dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|qq=}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from [[:en:Kitaura|Kitaura]], Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Katayamahiko-jina}}

The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]], and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This [[:en:Sangaku|sangaku]], dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|qq=}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from [[:en:Kitaura|Kitaura]], Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Katayamahiko-jina}}
