Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]], and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This [[:en:Sangaku|sangaku]], dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|qq=}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from [[:en:Kitaura|Kitaura]], Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Katayamahiko-jina}}

Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg |prefecture={{ill|Setouchi|qid=Q691691}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.69680555555556|N|134.10569444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]], and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This [[:en:Sangaku|sangaku]], dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|qq=|1=Kasaoka Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105729756}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from [[:en:Kitaura|Kitaura]], Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Katayamahiko-jina}} [[Category:qq]]

Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg |prefecture={{ill|Setouchi|qid=Q691691}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.69680555555556|N|134.10569444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]], and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This [[:en:Sangaku|sangaku]], dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|qq=|1=Kasaoka Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105729756}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from [[:en:Kitaura|Kitaura]], Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Katayamahiko-jina}} {{wikidata link|Q11569609}}

Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg |prefecture={{ill|Setouchi|qid=Q691691}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.69680555555556|N|134.10569444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]], and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This [[:en:Sangaku|sangaku]], dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|qq=|1=Kasaoka Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105729756}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from [[:en:Kitaura|Kitaura]], Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Katayamahiko-jina}} {{wikidata link|Q11569609}}

Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg |prefecture={{ill|Setouchi|WD=Q691691}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.69680555555556|N|134.10569444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]], and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This [[:en:Sangaku|sangaku]], dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|1=Kasaoka Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105729756}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from [[:en:Kitaura|Kitaura]], Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Katayamahiko-jina}} {{wikidata link|Q11569609}}

Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg |prefecture={{ill|Setouchi|WD=Q691691}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.69680555555556|N|134.10569444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|qq=}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for [[:en:Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto]] enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as [[:en:Oyamakui no Kami|Ōyamatsumi]], and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Okimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In [[:en:1873|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This [[:en:Sangaku|sangaku]], dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasaoka Village|1=Kasaoka Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105729756}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from [[:en:Kitaura|Kitaura]], Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Katayamahiko-jina}} {{wikidata link|Q11569609}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined in the Katayama area. The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]]

Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]]

It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[commons:Category:Katayamahiko-jina]][[ja:片山日子神社]] [[commons:Category:Katayamahiko-jina]][[ja:片山日子神社]] '''Katayama Hiko Shrine''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Bizen Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Oku District, Okayama|en|Oku District, Okayama|ja|邑久郡|zh|邑久郡|lt=Oku district|WD=Q7082713}} || {{ill|Katayama Hiko Shrine|lt=Katayamahiko Shrine|ja|片山日子神社|commons|Category:Katayamahiko-jina|WD=Q11569609}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.696818|134.105583|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183065 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11569609}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:2]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:3]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:4]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Katayamahiko Shrine |location=Setouchi City, Nagafune-cho, Haji 799, Okayama Prefecture |Position={{coord|34|41|48.47|N|134|6|20.51|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=Katayamahiko-no-Kami |Foundation=Traditionally Tenki 3 (1055) |ShrineRank=Shikinaisha, Gōsha |AnnualFestival=Spring Grand Festival: May 20
Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[commons:Category:Katayamahiko-jina]][[ja:片山日子神社]] [[commons:Category:Katayamahiko-jina]][[ja:片山日子神社]] '''Katayama Hiko Shrine''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Bizen Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Oku District, Okayama|en|Oku District, Okayama|ja|邑久郡|zh|邑久郡|lt=Oku district|WD=Q7082713}} || {{ill|Katayama Hiko Shrine|lt=Katayamahiko Shrine|ja|片山日子神社|commons|Category:Katayamahiko-jina|WD=Q11569609}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.696818|134.105583|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183065 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11569609}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:2]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:3]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:4]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Katayamahiko Shrine |location=Setouchi City, Nagafune-cho, Haji 799, Okayama Prefecture |Position={{coord|34|41|48.47|N|134|6|20.51|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=Katayamahiko-no-Kami |Foundation=Traditionally Tenki 3 (1055) |ShrineRank=Shikinaisha, Gōsha |AnnualFestival=Spring Grand Festival: May 20
Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Katayamahiko Shrine |location=Setouchi City, Nagafune-cho, Haji 799, Okayama Prefecture |Position={{coord|34|41|48.47|N|134|6|20.51|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=Katayamahiko-no-Kami |Foundation=Traditionally Tenki 3 (1055) |ShrineRank=Shikinaisha, Gōsha |AnnualFestival=Spring Grand Festival: May 20

The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Katayamahiko Shrine |location=Setouchi City, Nagafune-cho, Haji 799, Okayama Prefecture |Position={{coord|34|41|48.47|N|134|6|20.51|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity=Katayamahiko-no-Kami |Foundation=Traditionally Tenki 3 (1055) |ShrineRank=Shikinaisha, Gōsha |AnnualFestival=Spring Grand Festival: May 20

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." Autumn Grand Festival: October 10 |MainHall=Three-bay nagare-zukuri | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahiko jinja 01.jpg|260px|Katayamahiko Shrine]] }} '''Katayamahiko Shrine''' (片山日子神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami (片山日子命). It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a minor shrine under the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is Ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == According to the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine is listed as "Katayamahiko Shrine of the Junior Second Rank." It is considered equivalent to {{ill|Katayama Shrine (Kameyama)|ja|片山神社 (亀山市)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in Suzuka District, Ise Province. The enshrined deity is Katayamahiko-no-Kami, which, according to the shrine's tradition, is an abbreviated name for {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|ja|吉備津彦命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined in the Katayama area. Historical documents such as *Bizen Shikishakō* identify the deity as "Kata-yama Daimyōjin" or "Kata-yama Ōkami," while sources like *Jinjishiryō* refer to it as "Kamo Katayama-no-Mikoto." Other works, including *Bizen Kokushi* and *Jinja Kaku Roku,* attribute the deity as {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋神|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and *Kibi Onkōroku* identifies it as {{ill|Kamo no Okimi|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the exact founding date is unknown, it is traditionally believed that the shrine was originally located on Mount Kō (God Mountain) south of its current site. In the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically in Tenki 3 (1055), the shrine was relocated to its present location.*Biyō Kokushi* In {{ill|Meiji 6|ja|明治6th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1873), the shrine was designated as a Gōsha. In Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred rice and cloth from the government. == Cultural Properties == [[File:Katayamahiko jinja 10.jpg|thumb|200px|Mathematical votive tablet (sangaku)]] ;Designated Important Cultural Properties of Setouchi City * *Mathematical Votive Tablet of Katayamahiko Shrine* : This {{ill|sangaku|ja|算額|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, dated October of Meiji 6 (1873), features geometry problems solved by 16 disciples of the *Manjutei* mathematics school, founded by Nobunori Irie in {{ill|Kasaoka Village|ja|笠加村|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Mihara (present-day Minami, Setouchi City). The frame, adorned with carved dragons, was crafted by artisans Yūsensai and Kakuhō Inoue from Shirikai, Tamatsu Village (now Shirikai, Setouchi City). The dimensions are 180 cm by 182 cm, with zelkova wood for the frame and cedar for the board. Designated on March 31, 1990 (Heisei 2) by the former Nagafune Town. * *Danjiri (Festival Float) of Katayamahiko Shrine* : Constructed on May 15, 1882 (Meiji 15) by master carpenter Yoshie Chūkyū Katayama from {{ill|Kitaura|ja|北浦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kojima District (present-day Kitaura, Minami Ward, Okayama). The float was first paraded during the spring grand festival on May 19 of the same year and has been used during the autumn grand festival since the following year. It features all-zelkova construction, measuring 3.4 meters in height, 3.4 meters in length, and 2 meters in width. It was designated by the former Nagafune Town on April 16, 2002 (Heisei 14). == References == * Oku District Educational Association, *Oku District Gazetteer, Second Edition*, compiled by Kumao Kobayashi, 1915 * On-site informational signage == Notes == {{footnotes}} == External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=12066 Katayamahiko Shrine] - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Agency {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|片山日子神社|version=98523471|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hiko Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Bizen Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Setouchi]] [[Category:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hiko Shrine]] [[ja:片山日子神社]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
